response.justification: While Bogdan demonstrates strong software craftsmanship, system design skills, and an impressive track record with large-scale systems, he is a poor fit for this senior IDE/JVM role. His Java expertise is minimal and outdated (last Java work 6+ years ago at Altom), with no mention of Spring, Kotlin, or enterprise frameworks. Most critically, the role emphasizes IDE internals and language tooling expertise—areas entirely absent from his background. His recent 5 years have been focused on TypeScript/Node.js, moving away from JVM ecosystems. He would require extensive ramp-up in both Java and IDE tooling fundamentals, which is unrealistic for a senior-level position expected to take end-to-end ownership immediately.
strengths: Demonstrated software craftsmanship with strong TDD practices, CI/CD expertise, and ability to design extensible systems (evidenced by open-source projects and multiple senior roles)

Proven experience building and maintaining large-scale complex systems, including microservices at Dunnhumby (millions of requests/sec) and real-time platforms, showing capacity for system architecture
Excellent communication and knowledge-sharing skills, evidenced by multiple technical workshops, conference talks, and mentoring activities across diverse teams
concerns: Minimal and outdated Java/JVM expertise—last significant Java work was 6+ years ago at Altom (2016–2018) for robotic arms; no Spring, Hibernate, Quarkus, or modern enterprise framework experience
Zero IDE internals or language tooling experience; recent career trajectory is entirely TypeScript/Node.js (2020–present), moving away from JVM ecosystems rather than toward them
No exposure to build tools (Maven/Gradle), application servers, language servers, debuggers, or IDE plugin development—all core focus areas for this role

key_factors.factor: Strong software craftsmanship and system design skills
